headline.primary_headline: Why Haven't Neuropathy Sufferers Been Told These Facts?

headline.vsl_adaptation: If you're suffering from neuropathy - numbness, tingling, burning pain - why hasn't anyone told you about this drugless treatment? Three myths have kept you in the dark...

lead.opening_verbatim: Do you have any of the following symptoms? • Pins and needles feeling • Numbness in the hands or feet • Tingling or burning sensations • Weakness in the arms or legs • Sharp shooting or burning pains. If so, you may have a condition called peripheral neuropathy.
lead.transition_to_body: There's a NEW Drugless Solution Helping Neuropathy Sufferers.

mechanism.mechanism_description: Device analyzes the waveform of nerves, determines abnormalities, creates unique healing signals, administers them, and re-evaluates results. This happens 7.83 times every second during 30-minute treatments, helping natural nerve pathways between spine and extremities function again.

proof_elements.verbatim: a study on back pain in the British Medical Journal proved this myth false, showing that 75% of back pain sufferers who do nothing about it will have either pain or disability 12 months later
for every diabetic neuropathy patient, there are at least six more patients suffering with various neuropathies

key_sections.section_name: Myth #1: More Pills Are The Only Solution
key_sections.content_verbatim: A common treatment for many nerve problems is the 'take some pills and wait and see' method. While this may be necessary for temporary relief of severe symptoms, using them long term is no way to live. Some of the more common drugs given include pain pills, anti-seizure medications, and anti-depressants - all of which have serious side effects. Why not look for a drugless solution instead of just covering over the pain?

key_sections.section_name: Myth #2: Neuropathy is Only Found in Diabetic People
key_sections.content_verbatim: Diabetic patients are not the only group to suffer with this condition. Actually more neuropathy sufferers are non-diabetic than are, according to a recent 2009 study. 'Neuropathy is often misrepresented as only being diabetes related. However, this survey demonstrates that for every diabetic neuropathy patient, there are at least six more patients suffering with various neuropathies...' - Dr. Thomas H. Brannagan, III, medical advisor for The Neuropathy Association.

key_sections.section_name: Myth #3: Numbness and Tingling Will Go Away On Its Own
key_sections.content_verbatim: One of the biggest myths people believe about their numbness, tingling, and pain is that it goes away all by itself... without any treatment. But a study on back pain in the British Medical Journal proved this myth false, showing that 75% of back pain sufferers who do nothing about it will have either pain or disability 12 months later.

technology_description.how_it_works: Before each impulse is sent, it analyzes the waveform of your nerves, determines any abnormalities, creates the unique healing signal necessary, administers it, and then re-evaluates the result. This process happens 7.83 times every second or the 30 minute treatment.
technology_description.promise: helps the natural nerve pathways between your spine and feet (or hands) and can make your nerves functioning again

professional_endorsements.quote: The ReBuilder has helped our patients who have painful side effects from chemotherapy neuropathy so much...

professional_endorsements.source: John P. Hayes, Jr., DC, DABCO, Author - 'Beating Neuropathy'
professional_endorsements.quote: Previously, treating peripheral neuropathy patients hasn't been really successful... but I've never seen resolutions like we're seeing in these cases.

extraction_notes.quality_assessment: Solid myth-busting format with Gene Schwartz headline pattern
notable_elements: 'Why Haven't Sufferers Been Told' is classic Gene Schwartz standby headline
Three-myth structure creates multiple credibility-building moments
7.83 times per second is highly specific technology detail
Cancer Treatment Centers of America endorsement is premium institutional proof
extraction_notes.key_insight: The 'Why Haven't' headline immediately positions the reader as a victim of information suppression, priming them to receive the 'hidden facts' with gratitude rather than skepticism
